cards.front: What is swapping in operating systems?
cards.back: A process of exchanging data or pages between process memory and disk storage
Virtual memory swapping, Physical memory swapping
A technique of swapping a portion of memory to disk to free up memory for other processes

cards.front: What is the purpose of swapping in an operating system?
cards.back: To increase the available memory for running multiple processes and to prevent out-of-memory errors

cards.front: How does swapping improve system performance?
cards.back: By freeing up memory for other processes, allowing the system to optimize resource utilization and prevent slow performance due to low memory
